methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Elementl Power is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Chris Colbert that has raised $50M+. The valuation assessment draws on a comparative universe of 11 total peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private-stage nuclear energy firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and forms the sole quantitative anchor for this analysis. Using total raised of $50M as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $100M. The peer-median method was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-set comparables; while eight same-sector peers exist, the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide aggregation unreliable.

The triangulated range is $85M (low) to $115M (high), centered at $100M. This single-method derivation carries meaningful uncertainty. The narrow applicability reflects the frontier-stage nature of nuclear energy development, where heterogeneity in technology maturity, regulatory pathway, and capital efficiency creates sparse direct comparability. Key variables affecting valuation include deployment timeline, licensing progress, and realized engineering validation—none of which are quantified in available peer data. Further diligence on burn rate, capital efficiency relative to the $50M+ raised, and pathway to next funding event would substantially improve conviction.
